# UNITED STATES HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES — FINANCIAL DISCLOSURE STATEMENT

**FORM B** — For New Members, Candidates, and New Employees

Stamps: "HAND DELIVERED" / "LEGISLATIVE RESOURCE CENTER — 17 AUG 10 AM 9:15 — OFFICE OF THE CLERK, U.S. HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES". Handwritten initials "MC" next to (Office Use Only).

- **Name:** Rohit Khanna
- **Daytime Telephone:** 202) 225-2631
- **Filer Status:** [X] New Member of or Candidate for U.S. House of Representatives — State: CA, District: 17. Candidates – Date of Election: (blank)
- New Officer or Employee: not checked. Employing Office: (blank). Staff Filer Type (If Applicable): Shared (unchecked), Principal Assistant (unchecked)
- **Check if Amendment:** not checked
- **Period Covered:** January 1, 2016 to DECEMBER 31, 2016 (handwritten)
- Notice: "A $200 penalty shall be assessed against any individual who files more than 30 days late."

## PRELIMINARY INFORMATION — ANSWER EACH OF THESE QUESTIONS

| Q | Question | Answer |
|---|----------|--------|
| A | Did you, your spouse, or your dependent child: a. Own any reportable asset that was worth more than $1,000 at the end of the reporting period? or b. Receive more than $200 in unearned income from any reportable asset during the reporting period? | Yes [X] |
| C | Did you or your spouse have "earned" income (e.g., salaries, honoraria, or pension/IRA distributions) of $200 or more during the reporting period? | Yes [X] |
| D | Did you, your spouse, or your dependent child have any reportable liability (more than $10,000) at any point during the reporting period? | Yes [X] |
| E | Did you hold any reportable positions during the reporting period or in the current calendar year up through the date of filing? | Yes [X] |
| F | Did you have any reportable agreement or arrangement with an outside entity during the reporting period or in the current calendar year up through the date of filing? | No [X] |
| J | Did you receive compensation of more than $5,000 from a single source in the current year and two prior years? | No [X] |

ATTACH THE CORRESPONDING SCHEDULE IF YOU ANSWER "YES" — THIS FORM INCLUDES ONLY THE SCHEDULES THAT YOU ARE REQUIRED TO COMPLETE

## EXCLUSION OF SPOUSE, DEPENDENT, OR TRUST INFORMATION - ANSWER BOTH OF THESE QUESTIONS

| Question | Answer |
|----------|--------|
| TRUSTS – Details regarding "Qualified Blind Trusts" approved by the Committee on Ethics and certain other "excepted trusts" need not be disclosed. Have you excluded from this report details of such a trust that benefits you, your spouse, or dependent child? | No [X] |
| EXEMPTION – Have you excluded from this report any other assets, "unearned" income, or liabilities of a spouse or dependent child because they meet all three tests for exemption? Do not answer "yes" unless you have first consulted with the Committee on Ethics. | No [X] |

# SCHEDULE C - EARNED INCOME

Name: Rohit Khanna | Page ___ of ___

Instructions (printed): List the source, type, and amount of earned income from any source (other than the filer's current employment by the U.S. government) totaling $200 or more during the reporting period. For both the filer and filer's spouse, list the source and amount of any honoraria. List only the source for other spouse earned income exceeding $1,000. See examples below.

EXCLUDE: Military pay (such as National Guard or Reserve pay), federal retirement programs, and benefits received under the Social Security Act.

INCOME LIMITS and PROHIBITED INCOME: Be advised that the income limit and prohibited income may apply to you after you are on House payroll. The 2016 limit on outside earned income for Members and employees compensated at or above the "senior staff" rate was $27,495. The 2017 limit is $27,765. In addition, certain types of income (notably honoraria, director's fees, and payments for professional services involving a fiduciary relationship) are totally prohibited for Members and senior staff.

# SCHEDULE D - LIABILITIES

Name: Rohit Khanna | Page ___ of ___

Instructions (printed): Report liabilities of over $10,000 owed to any one creditor at any time during the reporting period by you, your spouse, or your dependent child. Mark the highest amount owed during the reporting period. New Members: Members are required to report all liabilities secured by real property including mortgages on their personal residence. Exclude: Any mortgage on your personal residence (unless you rent it out or are a Member); loans secured by automobiles, household furniture, or appliances; liabilities of a business in which you own an interest (unless you are personally liable); and liabilities owed to you by a spouse or the child, parent, or sibling of you or your spouse. Report a revolving charge account (i.e., credit card) only if the balance at the close of the reporting period exceeded $10,000. *Column K is for liabilities held solely by your spouse or dependent child.

Amount of Liability columns: A $10,001-$15,000; B $15,001-$50,000; C $50,001-$100,000; D $100,001-$250,000; E $250,001-$500,000; F $500,001-$1,000,000; G $1,000,001-$5,000,000; H $5,000,001-$25,000,000; I $25,000,001-$50,000,000; J Over $50,000,000; K Over $1,000,000* (Spouse/DC Liability).

- **REPORT TYPE:** [X] 2017 Annual (Due: May 15, 2018); Amendment [ ]; Termination [ ]; Date of Termination: blank

## PRELIMINARY INFORMATION — ANSWER EACH OF THESE QUESTIONS

| Q | Question | Answer |
|---|----------|--------|
| A | Did you, your spouse, or your dependent child: a. Own any reportable asset that was worth more than $1,000 at the end of the reporting period? or b. Receive more than $200 in unearned income from any reportable asset during the reporting period? | Yes |
| B | Did you, your spouse, or your dependent child purchase, sell, or exchange any securities or reportable real estate in a transaction exceeding $1,000 during the reporting period? | Yes |
| C | Did you or your spouse have "earned" income (e.g., salaries, honoraria, or pension/IRA distributions) of $200 or more during the reporting period? | Yes |
| D | Did you, your spouse, or your dependent child have any reportable liability (more than $10,000) at any point during the reporting period? | Yes |
| E | Did you hold any reportable positions during the reporting period or in the current calendar year up through the date of filing? | Yes |
| F | Did you have any reportable agreement or arrangement with an outside entity during the reporting period or in the current calendar year up through the date of filing? | No |
| G | Did you, your spouse, or your dependent child receive any reportable gift(s) totaling more than $390 in value from a single source during the reporting period? | No |
| H | Did you, your spouse, or your dependent child receive any reportable travel or reimbursements for travel totaling more than $390 in value from a single source during the reporting period? | Yes |
| I | Did any individual or organization make a donation to charity in lieu of paying you for a speech, appearance, or article during the reporting period? | No |

"ATTACH THE CORRESPONDING SCHEDULE IF YOU ANSWER \"YES\""

## IPO AND EXCLUSION OF SPOUSE, DEPENDENT, OR TRUST INFORMATION — ANSWER EACH OF THESE QUESTIONS

| Item | Question | Answer |
|------|----------|--------|
| IPO | Did you purchase any shares that were allocated as a part of an Initial Public Offering during the reporting period? If you answered "yes" to this question, please contact the Committee on Ethics for further guidance. | No |
| TRUSTS | Details regarding "Qualified Blind Trusts" approved by the Committee on Ethics and certain other "excepted trusts" need not be disclosed. Have you excluded from this report details of such a trust that benefits you, your spouse, or dependent child? | No |
| EXEMPTION | Have you excluded from this report any other assets, "unearned" income, transactions, or liabilities of a spouse or your dependent child because they meet all three tests for exemption? Do not answer "yes" unless you have first consulted with the Committee on Ethics. | No |

# United States House of Representatives 2021 Financial Disclosure Statement

Form A - For Use by Members, Officers, and Employees

Name: Rohit Khanna
Daytime Telephone: 202-225-2631

Filer Status: Member of the U.S. House of Representatives
State: CA
District: 17
Officer or Employee: unchecked
Staff Filer Type (If Applicable): Shared unchecked; Principal Assistant unchecked

Report Type: 2021 Annual (Due May 16, 2022) unchecked; Amendment checked; Termination unchecked; Date of Termination blank

## Preliminary Information

A. Did you, your spouse, or your dependent child: a. Own any reportable asset that was worth more than $1,000 at the end of the reporting period? b. Receive more than $200 in unearned income from any reportable asset during the reporting period? Yes checked; No unchecked

B. Did you, your spouse, or your dependent child purchase, sell, or exchange any reportable asset where a transaction exceeded $1,000 during the reporting period? Yes checked; No unchecked

C. Did you or your spouse have "earned" income (e.g., salaries, honoraria, or pension/IRA distributions) of $200 or more during the reporting period? Yes unchecked; No checked

D. Did you, your spouse, or your dependent child have any reportable liability (more than $10,000) at any point during the reporting period? Yes checked; No unchecked

E. Did you hold any reportable positions during the reporting period or in the current calendar year up through the date of filing? Yes checked; No unchecked

F. Did you have any reportable agreement or arrangement with an outside entity during the reporting period or in the current calendar year up through the date of filing? Yes unchecked; No checked

G. Did you, your spouse, or your dependent child receive any reportable gift(s) totaling more than $415 in value from a single source during the reporting period? Yes unchecked; No checked

H. Did you, your spouse, or your dependent child receive any reportable travel or reimbursements for travel totaling more than $415 in value from a single source during the reporting period? Yes unchecked; No checked

I. Did any individual or organization make a donation to charity in lieu of paying you for a speech, appearance, or article during the reporting period? Yes unchecked; No checked

ATTACH THE CORRESPONDING SCHEDULE IF YOU ANSWER "YES"

## IPO and Exclusion of Spouse, Dependent, or Trust Information

IPO - Did you purchase any shares that were allocated as a part of an Initial Public Offering during the reporting period? If you answered "yes" to this question, please contact the Committee on Ethics for further guidance. Yes unchecked; No checked

TRUSTS - Details regarding "Qualified Blind Trusts" approved by the Committee on Ethics and certain other "excepted trusts" need not be disclosed. Have you excluded from this report details of such a trust benefiting you, your spouse, or dependent child? Yes unchecked; No checked

EXEMPTION - Have you excluded from this report any other assets, "unearned" income, transactions, or liabilities of a spouse or your dependent child because they meet all three tests for exemption? Do not answer "yes" unless you have first consulted with the Committee on Ethics. Yes unchecked; No checked

Page: 6 of 6

Once you receive your K-1 form, you must amend your Statement if the category of value of your good faith estimate is different than the actual income received." |

Instructions (printed form text): Report liabilities of over $10,000 owed to any one creditor at any time during the reporting period by you, your spouse, or your dependent child. Mark the highest amount owed during the reporting period. Members are required to report all liabilities secured by real property including mortgages on their personal residence. Exclude: Any mortgage on personal residence (unless you rent it out or are a Member); loans secured by automobiles, household furniture, or appliances; liabilities of a business in which you own an interest (unless you are personally liable); and liabilities owed to you by a spouse or the child, parent, or sibling of you or your spouse. Report a revolving charge account (i.e., credit card) only if the balance at the close of the reporting period exceeded $10,000. *Column K is for liabilities held solely by your spouse or dependent child.

| Question | Answer |
|---|---|
| A. Did you, your spouse, or your dependent children: a. Own any reportable asset that was worth more than $1,000 at the end of the reporting period? or b. Receive more than $200 in unearned income from any reportable asset during the reporting period? | Yes [X] |
| B. Did you, your spouse, or your dependent children purchase, sell, or exchange any securities or reportable real estate in a transaction exceeding $1,000 during the reporting period? | Yes [X] |
| C. Did you or your spouse have "earned" income (e.g., salaries, honoraria, or pension/IRA distributions) of $200 or more during the reporting period? | No [X] |
| D. Did you, your spouse, or your dependent child have any reportable liability (more than $10,000) at any point during the reporting period? | No [X] |
| E. Did you hold any reportable positions during the reporting period or in the current calendar year up through the date of filing? | No [X] |
| F. Did you have any reportable agreement or arrangement with an outside entity during the reporting period or in the current calendar year up through the date of filing? | No [X] |
| G. Did you, your spouse, or your dependent children receive any reportable gift(s) totaling more than $480 in value from a single source during the reporting period? | No [X] |
| H. Did you, your spouse, or your dependent child receive any reportable travel or reimbursements for travel totaling more than $480 in value from a single source during the reporting period? | No [X] |
